qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unsafe deserialization in Tomcat Tribes clustering permits remote code execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Finding describes unsafe deserialization in XByteBuffer, while advisory describes padding oracle vulnerability in EncryptInterceptor. Different root cause mechanisms, different vulnerable components, different vulnerability classes (deserialization vs cryptographic padding oracle, CWE-502 vs CWE-209).

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Command injection in SSI Servlet exec directive permits arbitrary command execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Finding describes command injection in SSI servlet, while advisory describes padding oracle in EncryptInterceptor (Tomcat Tribes clustering component). Different root cause mechanisms, different vulnerable components, different vulnerability classes (command injection vs cryptographic padding oracle, CWE-78 vs CWE-209).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: Neither qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes a padding oracle vulnerability in Apache Tomcat's EncryptInterceptor component (CWE-209), which enables attackers to decrypt encrypted cluster communications by analyzing padding validation error responses. The two qualifying findings describe different vulnerabilities in different components: (1) deserialization RCE in XByteBuffer/Tribes module, and (2) command injection in the SSI servlet. Both findings have completely different root causes, vulnerable code locations, and vulnerability classes compared to the padding oracle vulnerability described in the advisory.
evidence: Advisory CVE-2026-29146 describes padding oracle vulnerability in EncryptInterceptor with CWE-209 (Information Exposure Through Error Message)
Advisory references point to EncryptInterceptor fixes in Tomcat security bulletins
Finding tribes-deserialization-rce targets XByteBuffer.deserialize() with unsafe ObjectInputStream fallback (CWE-502)
Finding ssi-command-injection targets SSIExec.process() with Runtime.exec() (CWE-78)
No code overlap between EncryptInterceptor and XByteBuffer or SSIExec components
Different vulnerability mechanisms: cryptographic padding oracle vs deserialization vs command injection
